fresh_facts.claim: Israel killed IRGC Intelligence Organization chief Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi in an airstrike on Monday, April 7, 2026.
U.S. and Israeli strikes killed 49 civilians and injured 58 in Iran over the past 24 hours as of April 7, 2026, according to HRANA.
The U.S. conducted a massive rescue operation for an F-15E crew shot down in southwest Iran on Friday, involving over 150 aircraft and 200 munitions.
Iran launched 14 missiles and 19 drones at the UAE in the past 24 hours, wounding 4 people.
Iranian missiles killed 4 people in Haifa, Israel on Monday, April 7, 2026, hitting a residential building.
Israel struck Iran's largest petrochemical facility in Eslamiyeh (50% of production capacity) and South Pars in Asaluyeh, causing tens of billions in estimated damage.
Iran rejected a U.S. ceasefire offer on April 6, 2026.
Pakistan proposed a 45-day ceasefire to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran rejected.
President Trump threatened to destroy Iranian infrastructure including power plants and bridges by Tuesday evening (April 7-8, 2026) if no deal is reached on the Strait of Hormuz.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed in strikes, according to reporting on April 6-7, 2026.
A Provisional Leadership Council has been established in Iran comprising President Masoud Pezeshkian, judiciary chief Gholamhossein Mohseni-Ejei, and cleric Alireza Arafi.
Mojtaba Khamenei (son of former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei) has become the new Supreme Leader following his father's death.
Iran appointed hardliner Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr as Supreme National Security Council chief on April 7, 2026.
The IRGC Intelligence Organization vowed major retaliation for Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi's death and issued a statement titled 'Operation Crushing Revenge' via Sepah News.
Iranian missiles and drones wounded 2 people in Jordan on April 7, 2026, with 1 missile and 1 drone not intercepted.
Iran released 20 cargo ships through the Strait of Hormuz starting Monday, April 7, 2026.
The U.S. presented a 15-point ceasefire plan to Iran via Pakistan covering Hormuz reopening, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback.
Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz and Prime Minister Netanyahu ordered continued strikes on Iranian national infrastructure on Monday, April 7, 2026.
Since the beginning of the conflict, Iran has launched 519 missiles, 26 cruise missiles, and 2,210 drones at Gulf states, resulting in 221 wounded and 13 killed.
Trump stated he postponed some military attacks on Iran for five days after claimed 'productive' talks led by envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.
Iran denied that negotiations with the U.S. occurred, contradicting Trump's claims of productive talks.
fresh_facts.source: Iranian government statements via wire service

fresh_facts.claim: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz described the killing of IRGC intelligence chief Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi as a major blow to Iran's war effort.
Hezbollah and Houthis joined Iran in striking Israel as of April 7, 2026.
Turkish President Erdoğan accused Israel of undermining peace efforts as of April 7, 2026.
Iran's Strait of Hormuz blockade and attacks on energy infrastructure have driven up global energy prices.
The Assembly of Experts in Iran, targeted in a March 3 Israeli strike in Qom, is delaying the succession process.
Wall Street rose as investors parsed U.S.-Iran talks on April 7, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: Day 41 sees conflicting succession reports with claims of Supreme Leader Khamenei's death and establishment of a Provisional Leadership Council, while military escalation continues with Israeli strikes on petrochemical infrastructure, Iranian regional attacks, and hardline leadership consolidation amid contradictory diplomatic signals including a U.S. 5-day bombing pause and ceasefire proposals that Iran has rejected.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei reportedly killed in strikes, with a Provisional Leadership Council established comprising President Pezeshkian, judiciary chief Mohseni-Ejei, and cleric Alireza Arafi, while Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly assumed the role of Supreme Leader.
